svg.title: Barista scheduling system — Telegram channel structure and data flow
svg.desc: Three Telegram channels: announcements (read-only), scheduling (interactive), and team chat (open). All feed into the scheduling engine which outputs the published roster.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What messages does the agent post in the announcements channel, and on what schedule?')
g.text: Pinned roster · weather alerts · shop news

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does a barista submit availability in the scheduling channel — what commands do they type?')
sendPrompt('Should the agent monitor the team chat channel for maintenance requests or swap requests?')
sendPrompt('What should the pinned agent info card say — name, commands, and rules?')
g.text: Who is the agent · available commands

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Walk me through the scheduling algorithm — how does it merge availability with the forecast?')
sendPrompt('What does the published roster message look like in the announcements channel?')
sendPrompt('What does the owner see before the roster is published — what does the HITL approval message look like?')
